senses.definition: Feelings or states in which someone becomes emotionally or socially separated from other people or from society.
examples: The novel explores different alienations experienced by young immigrants in a big city.
Modern technology can lead to new alienations, even as it connects us online.

senses.definition: Acts of transferring the ownership of property or rights to another person.
examples: The contract restricts alienations of the land without written consent.
Certain alienations of property may be invalid if made under pressure.

senses.definition: A person or thing that causes someone to feel distant, rejected, or separated from others.
examples: He was seen as an alienator of voters because of his harsh language.
Unfair school rules can become an alienator for many students.

senses.definition: The person who transfers property or rights to someone else.
examples: The alienator must sign the deed in front of a notary.
Information about the alienator and the buyer is listed in the contract.

senses.definition: People or things that make others feel separated, rejected, or disconnected.
examples: Social media trolls are powerful alienators in online communities.
Bad managers often become alienators who push talented staff away.

senses.definition: People who transfer property or rights to others.
examples: The law protects both alienators and alienees in such transactions.
Alienators are required to disclose all known defects in the property.

senses.definition: The quality of being foreign, strange, or not belonging.
examples: He felt the aliency of the customs in his new country.
The novel captures the sense of aliency that comes with exile.

senses.definition: To transfer the ownership of property or rights to another person.
examples: The landowner agreed to aliene part of the estate to his nephew.
Under the old law, she could not aliene the property without her husband's consent.

senses.definition: Transferred in ownership from one person to another.
examples: The estate was aliened to a distant relative.
Once aliened, the land could not be reclaimed by the family.

senses.definition: The person to whom property or a right is transferred.
examples: The alienee must register the deed within thirty days.
The rights of the alienee are protected under this statute.

senses.definition: People to whom property or rights are transferred.
examples: Future alienees should be informed about the legal limits on using the land.
The law sets out the protections given to alienees in such transactions.

senses.definition: A person who transfers property or rights to another person.
examples: The aliener must guarantee clear title to the property.
Aliener and alienee both signed the agreement.

senses.definition: People who transfer property or rights to others.
examples: The rights of alieners are defined clearly in the new code.
Alieners must disclose any existing mortgages on the land.

senses.definition: A foreigner living in a country, especially used in older legal or official language.
examples: As an alienicola, he did not have the right to own land in that kingdom.
The statute placed certain limits on alienicolae during wartime.

senses.definition: Foreigners living in a country, especially in older legal or official language.
examples: The law drew a sharp line between citizens and alienicolae.
Property restrictions applied mainly to alienicolae in the region.

senses.definition: Born in a foreign country; not native, especially in older legal use.
examples: Alienigenate subjects had limited rights under the old law.
He was described as an alienigenate merchant from overseas.

senses.definition: Making someone feel that they do not belong or are not welcome.
examples: By ignoring their feedback, the company risks aliening its most loyal customers.
His rude comments are aliening his friends.

senses.definition: Transferring property or rights to another person.
examples: She is aliening half of her share in the company.
Aliening the land without approval would be a breach of the agreement.

senses.definition: The study and treatment of mental illness, especially in older medical language.
examples: He wrote a history of alienism in the nineteenth century.
Alienism developed into modern psychiatry over time.

senses.definition: Theories, practices, or references related to the old study of mental illness called alienism.
examples: The article traces various alienisms that influenced early psychiatric care.
These alienisms are now seen as outdated and sometimes harmful.

senses.definition: A doctor who treats mental illness, especially in older use.
examples: The court asked an alienist to examine the defendant.
In the novel, an alienist studies the mind of a serial killer.

senses.definition: Doctors who treat mental illness, especially in older language.
examples: In the late nineteenth century, alienists began to study abnormal behavior scientifically.
Alienists were often called to give expert testimony in court cases.

senses.definition: To make something or someone seem foreign, strange, or distant.
examples: Using too much jargon can alienize the text for general readers.
The new policy may alienize many long-term employees.

senses.definition: In a foreign, strange, or unfamiliar way.
examples: The room felt alienly silent after they left.
She moved through the crowd almost alienly, as if she did not belong there.

senses.definition: The quality of being foreign, unfamiliar, or very different.
examples: She was struck by the alienness of the landscape.
The film emphasizes the alienness of life on another planet.

senses.definition: Different kinds or instances of being foreign or unfamiliar.
examples: The writer explores various aliennesses felt by migrants in the city.
These aliennesses can be both exciting and frightening.

senses.definition: A person who transfers property or a right to another person.
examples: The alienor is responsible for all costs up to the date of transfer.
Details of the alienor must be recorded in the deed.

senses.definition: People who transfer property or rights to others.
examples: The law protects both alienors and alienees in such transactions.
Alienors must provide accurate information about the property.

senses.definition: Imaginary creatures from another planet.
examples: The movie is about aliens invading Earth.
Children drew pictures of green aliens in their art class.

senses.definition: People who are not citizens of the country where they are living or staying.
examples: The law refers to foreign nationals as aliens.
Resident aliens must renew their permits regularly.

senses.definition: The condition of being a foreigner or not belonging to a place.
examples: He felt a constant alienship in his adopted country.
Alienship can shape a person's identity in powerful ways.

senses.definition: An enzyme that helps break chemical bonds in certain ester compounds, especially in aliphatic esters.
examples: Researchers measured the activity of aliesterase in the liver tissue.
Aliesterase plays a role in the hydrolysis of specific drugs.

senses.definition: Related to a wing-shaped part of the ethmoid bone in the skull.
examples: The surgeon noted a fracture in the aliethmoid region.
Aliethmoid structures are visible in certain skull diagrams.

senses.definition: Relating to the aliethmoid part of the ethmoid bone.
examples: The aliethmoidal surface was carefully cleaned during the operation.
Aliethmoidal cells can be affected in certain sinus diseases.

senses.definition: The first letter of the Arabic alphabet.
examples: The teacher wrote alif on the board for the students to copy.
In Arabic, words like 'adam' begin with alif.

senses.definition: Short for artificial life, the study of life-like systems created by humans, often in computers or robots.
examples: She is interested in alife and complex adaptive systems.
The conference included a session on alife simulations.

senses.definition: Having wings or wing-like parts.
examples: Aliferous insects appeared in the fossil record at this time.
The artist drew an aliferous creature rising into the sky.

senses.definition: Shaped like a wing.
examples: The shell has an aliform extension on each side.
Aliform bones help support the structure of the skull.

senses.definition: The first letters of the Arabic alphabet, in plural.
examples: The children practiced writing alifs across the page.
The exercise asked learners to circle all the alifs in the text.

senses.definition: An old or nonstandard spelling of alligator.
examples: The word 'aligarta' appears in several seventeenth-century texts.
In the document, an 'aligarta' is described lurking in the river.

senses.definition: Old or nonstandard spellings of alligators.
examples: The explorers wrote about 'aligartas' in the swamps.
In the story, several aligartas surround the boat.

senses.definition: Having wings; winged.
examples: In the painting, aligerous angels fly above the city.
The poet describes an aligerous spirit moving through the forest.

senses.definition: To get out of a vehicle such as a bus, train, or horse.
examples: Passengers should alight at the next stop.
She alighted from the train and waved goodbye.

senses.definition: To land gently on something, especially a bird or insect.
examples: A butterfly alighted on her hand.
The bird alighted on the branch above us.

senses.definition: Burning or on fire.
examples: The car was alight when the firefighters arrived.
Several houses were set alight during the riots.

senses.definition: Full of light, excitement, or strong emotion.
examples: Her face was alight with joy.
The children's eyes were alight with excitement.

senses.definition: Got off a vehicle such as a bus, train, or horse.
examples: We alighted at the wrong station by mistake.
She alighted from the taxi and ran inside.

senses.definition: Landed gently on something, especially a bird or insect.
examples: A small bird alighted on the window ledge.
The butterfly alighted briefly on the flower.

senses.definition: To light up or brighten something.
examples: The candles alightened the dark hall.
Her smile seemed to alighten the whole room.

senses.definition: Getting off a vehicle such as a bus, train, or horse.
examples: Passengers alighting here should use the rear door.
Alighting from the bus, he checked the street name.

senses.definition: Landing gently on something, especially a bird or insect.
examples: We watched the birds alighting on the telephone wires.
Bees were alighting on every flower in the garden.

senses.definition: The act of getting off a vehicle, especially in formal or older use.
examples: Alightment should be done only at designated stops.
The rules of alightment are explained in the safety leaflet.

senses.definition: Events or occasions of getting off vehicles, especially in formal or older use.
examples: The timetable lists all stops and alightments along the route.
Unsafe alightments can lead to accidents.

senses.definition: Gets off a vehicle such as a bus, train, or horse.
examples: She always alights at the last stop.
The driver waits until everyone alights safely.

senses.definition: Lands gently on something, especially a bird or insect.
examples: A crow alights on the fence every morning.
The butterfly alights and then quickly flies away.

senses.definition: To put things in a straight line or correct position in relation to each other.
examples: Please align the text in the center of the page.
The mechanic needs to align the wheels of the car.

senses.definition: To support or agree with a person, group, or idea.
examples: The company decided to align itself with environmental groups.
He tends to align with more conservative views.

senses.definition: Arranged in a straight line or in the correct position.
examples: The chairs were neatly aligned along the wall.
Make sure the two parts are properly aligned before tightening the screws.

senses.definition: In agreement or harmony with something such as values, goals, or standards.
examples: Our strategy is aligned with our long-term vision.
Their interests are closely aligned with ours.

senses.definition: Arranged or positioned in a straight line, or brought into agreement with something.
examples: They aligned the shelves with the window.
The project aligned well with the company's mission.

senses.definition: A person or thing that arranges something in a straight line or correct position.
examples: The technician is an expert aligner of satellite dishes.
Use a laser aligner to position the beams accurately.

senses.definition: A clear plastic dental device used to move teeth into a better position.
examples: She wears a removable aligner to straighten her teeth.
The dentist recommended a series of aligners instead of metal braces.

senses.definition: People or tools that arrange things in a straight line or correct position.
examples: The company hired specialist aligners for the new machinery.
Laser aligners are used in construction to ensure accuracy.

senses.definition: Clear plastic dental devices used to move teeth into better positions.
examples: She changed her aligners every two weeks.
Clear aligners are popular because they are almost invisible.

senses.definition: Putting things in a straight line or correct position.
examples: He spent the afternoon aligning the shelves on the wall.
Aligning the printer heads can improve print quality.

senses.definition: Bringing people, plans, or ideas into agreement or harmony.
examples: The manager is aligning the team's goals with the company's strategy.
Aligning your actions with your values can make decisions easier.
